positive_description: Extremely subtle effect, possibly affecting nasolabial or philtrum area. At +3.0 the midface below the nose may show a barely perceptible change in contour. The rest of the face is unchanged. At +1.5, identical to neutral.
negative_description: At -3.0 the opposite pole shows an equally minimal change in the nose-to-lip region. The effect is below the threshold of reliable visual detection at these magnitudes.
symmetry_notes: Appears symmetric. No left-right asymmetry detected, though the effect is too small for reliable assessment.
artifact_notes: No artifacts at any tested value. Negligible vertex displacement.
prose: Beta28 is another very low-variance component whose visual effect at +/-3.0 is at or below the detection threshold. Careful side-by-side comparison of the extreme renders hints at a possible change in the nasolabial or philtrum region — the area between the nose and upper lip — but the magnitude is too small to characterize with any certainty. The renders are functionally identical across the entire tested range. This component captures real population variation in a facial region that is anatomically distinctive (philtrum shape varies significantly across individuals) but at this PCA rank the captured variance is too small to produce visible effects at practical magnitudes.
